title: Spec-decode coverage + decode tok/s across the fleet
subtitle: 2x R9700 TP=2, --speculative-attention-mode decode, full 256K unless noted. Bars = AWQ production ships; FP8-target spec measured separately 2026-05-31 (see fp8-comparison.json)
note: Single-bar = best production spec-decode decode tok/s (target + draft + --speculative-attention-mode decode @ TP2). speedup x is over the family's ~22 tok/s no-spec baseline. 'untested' = a viable draft path exists but unbenched; 'blocked' = no working draft yet, with the blocker. As blocked/untested models get fixed, flip status->working + fill spec_toks and regenerate — no model should sit at a bare N/A. Data sourced from the README spec-decode summary + coverage map. FP8-TARGET note (2026-05-31, post-iommu=pt): these bars are AWQ-target production numbers (short-ctx spec works without iommu=pt, so they stand). FP8 targets differ: Coder-30B-FP8+EAGLE3 = ~86 tok/s coherent (256K served, tiny 361MB draft); Qwen3.6-35B-FP8+DFlash = ~45 tok/s coherent AT 256K (accept ~3.9) via --chunked-prefill 2048 — the prior 'OOMs@256K' was a prefill-transient, not a draft wall (KV pool 782K was never the limit). So BOTH A3B-MoE FP8 models do FP8+draft@256K (Coder-30B EAGLE3 ~86, Qwen3.6-35B DFlash ~45). The 80 here is the AWQ ship (faster — Marlin-class int4 verify); FP8 trails AWQ on absolute tok/s but both reach 256K.
